summary: Package fetches executable payload from anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket
details: dist/bootstrap.js fetches content from https://pub-046c52795b9445cd9f5cc5cb21b9d59f.r2.dev — an anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket — and references process.env in the same file (L11, L14). This R2 host pattern (pub-<32hex>.r2.dev) matches the known-bad infrastructure used by the @chahuadev/* npm cluster to drop platform-native binaries with no publisher accountability, no version pinning, and no integrity verification. The bucket owner is anonymous and the contents are mutable, so any installer that loads bootstrap.js executes whatever bytes the bucket currently serves. A second file, dist/api/dadosabertos.js, performs HTTPS calls to apidadosabertos.saude.gov.br (the Brazilian Ministry of Health open-data API), which is consistent with the package's stated MCP-CNES purpose, but does not justify or explain the separate R2 fetch in bootstrap.js. The combination of (a) fetch from an anonymous, mutable, non-publisher host and (b) environment-variable access in the same bootstrap module is the install-/load-time dropper fingerprint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
